description: PRD-1 tracer-bullet vertical slice. Ten linear-dependency slices that prove the v0 architecture end-to-end: signup -> upload PDF -> AI drafts L2 checklist with citations -> InboxItem -> accept with audit. See docs/prd-1-tracer-bullet.md and docs/architecture.md for full context. Two HITL gates (Slice 06: first AI output review; Slice 08: design review against Blueprint mockup) require human approval before marking passes:true. Slice 01 also HITL because it needs account creation (Supabase/Vercel/Railway/Google AI Studio); the human should complete it manually and mark passes:true before starting the loop.

userStories.title: Repo + deploy hello-world
userStories.description: Bootstrap the deploy pipeline: Next.js on Vercel, FastAPI on Railway, Supabase project, GitHub Actions CI, ESLint context-boundaries stub. HITL: requires account creation; do this manually before starting the autonomous loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Vercel URL returns 200 with default Next.js page
Railway /health endpoint returns {"status":"ok"}
GitHub Actions runs typecheck + ESLint + pytest on every PR; failures block merge
eslint-plugin-boundaries configured with stub contexts identity/ and commissioning/ under src/contexts/; cross-context internal imports fail lint
README documents local dev setup and required env vars
CI secrets present: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, DATABASE_URL, DATABASE_SECRET, GEMINI_API_KEY
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/4. Accounts already exist; credentials are in .env.local. Agent should scaffold Next.js frontend, FastAPI backend, GitHub Actions CI, ESLint boundaries config, and README. Deploy to Vercel using VERCEL_TOKEN env var, deploy to Railway using RAILWAY_TOKEN env var. Set GitHub Actions secrets using gh CLI. Do not attempt to create new accounts.

userStories.title: Sign-in to a project (RLS proven)
userStories.description: Supabase Auth + minimum party model (orgs, users, memberships, projects, participations, assignments, discipline_scopes) + invite-by-email + RLS isolation across orgs.
acceptanceCriteria: New user signs up via email; lands signed in
User creates Org, then Project, then invites a teammate by email; invitee lands signed in with role + project pre-assigned
Project creation auto-creates one DisciplineScope named 'Mechanical'
Automated test: user_a in org_a cannot read or modify project_b in org_b via any API path (REST + Supabase client)
All new tables have RLS enabled
Migration files checked in
Two roles seeded: OCA and cx_engineer
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/5. Blocked by Slice-01.

userStories.title: Upload a PDF and see status
userStories.description: PDF upload flow with Supabase Storage, Document table, transactional outbox event emission, live status via Supabase Realtime.
acceptanceCriteria: OCA can upload a PDF on the project page; file appears in documents list
Non-PDF uploads rejected with clear error before any DB write
Files >25MB rejected with clear error before storage upload
On success: documents row created with status='uploaded', outbox row created with event_type='document_uploaded', both in same transaction
UI status pill updates uploaded -> processing -> indexed via Realtime without page refresh
RLS verified: user from another org cannot read this document or its storage object
Migrations checked in
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/6. Blocked by Slice-02.

userStories.title: Outbox dispatcher across two processes
userStories.description: Transactional Outbox pattern with Postgres NOTIFY/LISTEN + fallback poll, two consumers (Next.js + Python), idempotency via outbox_dispatches table. Survives worker crashes; exactly-once delivery per subscriber.
acceptanceCriteria: Inserting an outbox row causes both Next.js and Python workers to log handling within 2s (LISTEN path)
Fallback poll picks up missed NOTIFY events within 30s
Killing a worker mid-event and restarting results in exactly-once processing (verified by idempotency test)
outbox_dispatches row exists for every successful process
Unknown event_type is logged and marked dispatched (no queue blockage)
Integration test: 100 concurrent inserts; all dispatched exactly once to each subscriber
Subscriber registry pattern in place: contexts register (event_type, handler) at startup
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/7. Blocked by Slice-03. No user-visible behavior; the demo is the integration test output.

userStories.title: PDF ingestion to ExtractedSpec
userStories.description: Python ingestion pipeline: detector classifies doc type, chunker embeds into pgvector, one DSPy extractor produces ExtractedSpec for submittal-cut-sheet doc type. Consumes DocumentUploaded; emits DocumentIndexed.
acceptanceCriteria: Uploading a submittal-cut-sheet PDF results in documents.status='indexed' within 60s
document_chunks rows exist with non-null pgvector embeddings and page/coords metadata
extracted_specs row exists with equipment_type, manufacturer, model, design_specs
DocumentIndexed event present in outbox
Non-submittal PDF results in documents.status='failed' with clear failure_reason
Detector is a typed DSPy module returning an enum (no string parsing)
pgvector similarity query against document_chunks returns relevant chunks for a known-related fixture query
Subscriber registered in Slice-04 dispatcher; consumes DocumentUploaded idempotently
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/8. Blocked by Slice-04.

userStories.title: AI drafts checklist from ExtractedSpec
userStories.description: CxExecutionAgent (LangGraph) consumes DocumentIndexed, retrieves ExtractedSpec + chunks, runs DSPy GenerateL2Checklist module that REQUIRES citations, produces draft TestProcedureInstance. AgentRun records full audit. Refusal path when confidence too low. HITL: human reviews 3+ real submittal outputs before marking done.
acceptanceCriteria: Full pipeline (Slices 3-6) on a real submittal PDF produces a test_procedure_instances row with status='draft', actor_type='ai', body containing >=5 checklist steps
At least one citations row links the draft to a document_chunks row with valid page/coords
agent_runs row captures input, output, tool_calls, token_cost, latency_ms, model_version
Zero-high-confidence retrieval results in agent REFUSAL: no draft created; AIRefusal event emitted with reason
DSPy signature for GenerateL2Checklist declares citations REQUIRED; test fixture proves DSPy retries or fails on missing citations
AgentRunCompleted event emitted to outbox on success
HUMAN REVIEW: 3+ real submittals processed; engineer + product reviewer confirm drafted checklists are customer-credible. Do not mark passes:true until human signs off.
userStories.notes: HITL. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/9. Blocked by Slice-05. The agent loop should build the code, but a human must review real AI outputs before marking this slice complete.

userStories.title: Inbox-as-home receives the draft
userStories.description: Auto-subscribe OCA to AgentRunCompleted events for own runs; materialize as InboxItem; render Inbox-as-home with Realtime push. Daily bucket only. Distinct treatment for AI refusal events.
acceptanceCriteria: On UserProjectAssignment creation, subscriptions row auto-created for OCA listening to agent_run.completed
After Slice-06 produces a draft, InboxItem row appears within 5s with action_state='pending'
OCA's home shows Inbox view with the new InboxItem card: AI badge, asset tag, source doc title, 'Review draft' CTA
InboxItem appears via Realtime without page refresh
AI refusal surfaces as a distinct InboxItem variant ('AI needs more info') with reason + 'Upload more docs' CTA
cx_engineer does NOT see OCA's InboxItem (RLS + per-user subscription scope verified)
Empty state UI: 'Nothing needs your attention. Upload a document to get started.'
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/10. Blocked by Slice-06. Net-new design (not in Blueprint mockup) — keep minimal: daily bucket, in-app only, no digest/escalation.

userStories.title: Entity detail page + AI Chat drawer (Blueprint visual)
userStories.description: Canonical three-panel layout per architecture §4.2. Citation chips deep-link to PDF page. AI Chat drawer auto-scoped to entity. Cmd-K palette stub. Blueprint visual direction (light, Archivo + JetBrains Mono, blue+cream palette). HITL: design review against frontend-design/ mockup before marking done.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an InboxItem lands on the entity-detail page for that TestProcedureInstance
Desktop >=1024px: three panels (left nav, center entity, right drawer); <1024px reflows to single-scroll with drawer as bottom sheet
Each checklist step shows numbered ID (1.1, 1.2) in JetBrains Mono with at least one citation chip
Clicking a citation chip opens source PDF at correct page coordinate
AI Chat tab is the default open tab in the right drawer
Asking 'why this step?' returns a grounded answer with at least one new inline citation
Activity tab shows AgentRun entry with AI lightning-bolt glyph distinguishing AI from human entries
Cmd-K opens a palette; matches Projects + Documents by name
Visual tokens (color, typography, glyphs, state badge pills) match Blueprint mockup at frontend-design/
HUMAN REVIEW: product + design reviewer approve rendered page side-by-side with Blueprint mockup before marking passes:true
userStories.notes: HITL. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/11. Blocked by Slice-07. Most visible UI surface — pause for design review.

userStories.title: Accept draft (state transition + audit + feedback)
userStories.description: Accept button (role-gated to OCA) transitions TestProcedureInstance draft -> active in one tx. AuditLogEntry with actor_type='human', confirmed_ai_run_id populated. FeedbackRecord thumb up/down on every AgentRun card. Refusal path-forward UX.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept button visible to OCA on draft TestProcedureInstance; hidden from cx_engineer
Click transitions status: draft -> active in single DB transaction
audit_log_entries row written: actor_type='human', confirmed_ai_run_id populated with AgentRun id
InboxItem marked action_state='acted' and removed from daily Inbox view
Activity tab shows: 'human · OCA accepted at <timestamp> · confirming AI draft from <agent_run_id>'
Thumb-up / thumb-down render on every AgentRun card in AI Chat drawer
Thumb-down opens 'what's wrong?' textarea; submit writes feedback_records row
Refusal-variant UI: when AgentRun.status='refused', entity detail shows refusal card with reason + 'provide more info' CTA
TestProcedureInstanceActivated event present in outbox after acceptance
Integration test: full path Slices 3-9 produces an active TestProcedureInstance with correct audit chain
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/12. Blocked by Slice-08.

userStories.title: EvalRun harness gates CI
userStories.description: Ten seed fixtures + pytest harness + GitHub Action that blocks PR on AI quality regression. The data-network-effect seed: every pilot correction becomes a future fixture.
acceptanceCriteria: Ten fixtures in ai_service/eval/fixtures/, each with a real submittal PDF + hand-curated expected.json (expected_extracted_spec, expected_min_citations, expected_steps_count_range)
pytest ai_service/eval/test_cx_execution.py runs locally and reports per-fixture pass/fail
eval_runs row written for every fixture x run
GitHub Action 'ai-eval' runs on every PR touching ai_service/**, prompts/**, or fixtures/
Intentionally regressing a prompt (e.g., removing citation requirement) causes ai-eval job to fail
Baseline stored as JSON in repo; regression = >10% drop in pass rate or citation recall vs main baseline
README in ai_service/eval/ documents add-fixture workflow
userStories.notes: AFK. GitHub issue: https://github.com/shlokpatel1998/CXPro/issues/13. Blocked by Slice-09. Final slice — after this, v0 demo flow is shippable and quality-gated.
